Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/27948/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/27948/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

(defect) We should not add new ioctls for in kernel communication. With getstripe it's different: we are passing an ioctl from userspace down to LOV. But this is too complicated for what's needed.

Please add a new cl_object function
 
bool cl_object_has_ldlm_locks(struct cl_object *obj);

or similar.

(1 comment)

After discussion with Jinshan I will not hold this up.

Let's add a new series of ioctls for use with cl_object_ioctl() and not pass any of the existing (lustre_ioctl.h,...) ioctls to cl_object_ioctl().

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

I don't understand.  Why is are we removing reasonably clear operations like "cbdata" and "change_cbdata" and then creating a new layer of ioctl obfuscation?

We are supposed to be simplifying here!

Also, there I don't see any tests included as part of the new interface.

parents.subject: LU-5971 llite: move vvp_io functions to vvp_io.c

commit.subject: LU-5823 clio: add cl_object_find_cbdata()
commit.message:
LU-5823 clio: add cl_object_find_cbdata()

* Delete obsolete obd_ops::o_find_cbdata interface.
* Delete obsolete obd_ops::o_change_cbdata interface.
* Add cl_object_find_cbdata().

Signed-off-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam.xu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I2e64e2e9a112783cb5c66bf4580fd1aec794417b
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/12494
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
